competitors.title: OpenAI Chat Completions vs Responses vs Assistants 2026
key_points: Chat Completions is stateless — you send the full message history every request, you own persistence
Responses API (launched early 2025) maintains state server-side — previous_response_id chains turns without resending history
Assistants API manages Threads — persistent conversation objects with full OpenAI-managed lifecycle, but being sunset in H1 2026
Responses API supports built-in tool calls — web search, file search, computer use as first-class tools
Chat Completions is cheapest (no state overhead), Responses API is mid-cost, Assistants API is most expensive
For simple chat: Chat Completions; for multi-step agentic flows with state: Responses API; for document Q&A: Assistants (migrating to Responses)
Migration from Chat Completions to Responses is cleanest path — same model IDs, same tool definitions, can be done incrementally
Responses API deprecated Assistants API patterns — OpenAI converging on Responses as primary stateful API

competitors.title: Open Responses vs. Chat Completion: A new era for AI apps
key_points: OpenAI transitioned from Chat Completions to Responses API in March 2025
Open Responses specification created with Nvidia, Vercel, OpenRouter, Hugging Face, LM Studio, Ollama, and vLLM for multi-provider interoperability
Key problems solved: manual state management, tool orchestration complexity, lost reasoning context between turns, no built-in capabilities
Chat Completions requires client-side while loops, JSON parsing, retry handling; Responses API moves control logic server-side
Responses API uses semantic streaming events (tool.start, tool.end, content.add) vs Chat Completions raw token deltas
Reasoning tokens from o3 and o4-mini are preserved between turns in Responses API but discarded in Chat Completions
Network traffic significantly reduced: 1 request for N steps via Responses vs N round-trips for N steps via Chat Completions
Open Responses marks the end of the Chatbot Era and beginning of the Agentic Era

competitors.title: OpenAI API: Responses vs. Chat Completions
key_points: Chat Completions API will continue to be supported indefinitely; Assistants API being sunset in H1 2026
Most important feature of Responses API is server-side conversation state management via previous_response_id
Built-in tools: web_search_preview ($25-50/1K queries), file_search ($2.50/1K queries, $0.10/GB/day storage), computer_use_preview
Responses API supports HTML form encoding in addition to JSON for simpler curl usage
The openai-python repository had a giant commit updating ALL example code to Responses API patterns
Responses API feels more natural than Assistants API which required thinking in threads, messages, and runs

competitors.title: OpenAI Function Calling: Complete Guide for Agent Builders (2026)
key_points: Function calling is a 5-step loop: define tools → send request → model returns tool_calls → execute and return results → model generates final response
Strict mode (strict: true) enables constrained decoding for 100% schema compliance at generation level, recommended for all production
Parallel tool calls: model returns multiple function_calls in one response, execute all in parallel, send all results back in one request
Structured outputs vs function calling are different features: function calling invokes tools, structured outputs formats the response as JSON
MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration: function calling defines tool schemas per-request, MCP provides tool discovery dynamically
For dependent operations set parallel_tool_calls: false; reasoning models (o3, o4-mini) naturally produce sequential calls

stats.fact: Responses API launched March 2025; Assistants API sunsetting H1 2026
Web search tool pricing: $25-50 per 1,000 queries depending on model and search context size
File search tool: $2.50 per 1,000 queries, $0.10/GB/day storage (first GB free)
Open Responses specification backed by Nvidia, Vercel, OpenRouter, Hugging Face, LM Studio, Ollama, vLLM, Azure OpenAI
Chat Completions can be 5x more expensive for long conversations — 10-turn conversation resends full history each time vs single delta with Responses API
Strict mode achieves 100% schema compliance via constrained decoding at token generation level
Ollama added Open Responses support in v0.13.3 (non-stateful flavor), vLLM has full compatible server
Code Interpreter billed per session hour at flat rate; File Search at $0.10/1,000 queries on Assistants API
Reasoning tokens from o3 and o4-mini are preserved between turns in Responses API but discarded in Chat Completions, degrading agentic performance

angles: Migrating from Chat Completions to Responses API: a practical step-by-step guide for Python developers
Building stateful multi-turn AI agents with previous_response_id — no more manual history management
Built-in tools that replace custom infrastructure: web search, file search, and computer use in one API call
The Open Responses specification: write once, run on any provider (OpenAI, Azure, Ollama, vLLM, Hugging Face)
Cost comparison: when Chat Completions is actually cheaper vs when Responses API saves money on long conversations
Function calling patterns with the Responses API: strict mode, parallel execution, and the agentic loop
Why Assistants API is being sunset and how to migrate Threads/Runs code to Responses API patterns
